title: Mafia Island Marine Park (Prohibited and Regulated Activities) Regulations, 2006
title_en: Mafia Island Marine Park (Prohibited and Regulated Activities) Regulations, 2006

provisions.title: Citation and date of commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Mafia Island Marine Park (Prohibited and Regulated Activities) Regulations, 2006 and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations:— " Act " means the Marine Parks and Reserves Act . " chemically polluted " means mixed with an industrial or consumer chemical or chemical product in any form, whether manufactured or obtained from nature; " coral " means living or dead calcareous skeleton secreted by marine polyps coelenterates of class Anthrozoa deposited in various forms; " core zone " means a no-take zone area within the marine park designated as such by the Minister in the zoning scheme in regulations made under the Act ; " general use zone " means an area within the marine park which is not a core zone or a specified use zone; " harpoon " means a spear-like gun with a rope attached for catching whales or fish; " high water mark " means the coast baseline drawn from an average high tide elevation as marked on large scale charts maintained by the Warden and officially recognized by the Government; " high water mark " means the coast baseline drawn from an average high tide elevation as marked on large scale charts maintained by the Warden and officially recognized by the Government; " industrial development " means manufacturing or production establishment other than domestic; " MIMP " means the Mafia Island Marine Park so declared under to section 9 of the Act ; " National Environment Management Council " means the Council established by the Environment Management Act , 2004 for the oversight of environmental matters in Mainland Tanzania; " neap tide " means a tide having the lowest difference between high and low water levels and occurring at the first and third quarters of the moon; " port development " means construction or upgrading of a harbour at which ships take on or offload cargo; " pull net " means a fishing net or gear made of thread or wire or any other material designed to be used for the purpose of collecting, capturing, gathering or killing of aquatic flora and fauna by pulling ground ropes; " resident " means a person ordinarily resident in the marine park; " spear gun " means a gun which discharges a missile like spear; " specified use zone " means an area within the marine park designated as such by the Minister in the zoning scheme in regulations made under the Act ; " stakeholder " means a person or institution having an interest in or is affected by the activities or proceedings conducted in the marine park under the Act ; and " Warden " means a person having charge and overall management responsibility of the Mafia Island marine park under the Act . Part II – Prohibited activities

provisions.title: Prohibited activities
provisions.content: No person shall within the MIMP carry on any of the activities specified in this regulation, namely— (a) the extraction of living resources— (i) by use of beach-seine nets including nets known locally as juya, juya la kojani and kavogo juya la kusini and kokoro; (ii) by Use of any pull nets with stretched-meshsize of less than 2.5 inches, including use of nets known locally as tandio and mtando; (iii) subject to provision of regulation 5 , by using a purse seine (fishing) net (nyavu za dagaa nocturnally with light attraction; (iv) through any process involving mechanical damage to, or breakage of corals or other benthic habitat or organisms, whether by hand or by use of poles or other implements; (vi) by using explosives; [Please note: Numbering as in original.] (vii) by trawling; (viii) by using any spear-gun or harpoon propelled in any way except by hand, other than for octopus fishing; (ix) by using chemicals or poisons; (x) by using scuba other than for research purposes specifically approved by the Warden ; (xi) involving the deliberate killing of dugong, dolphin and turtles, including removal of turtle eggs; and (xii) by using fishing gear or methods similar to those described in this sub regulation. (b) pursuant to regulation 15 the Warden may grant permission for the purpose of research activities specified under sub-paragraphs (i) , (ii) , (iii) , (iv) , (i) , and (ix) (c) the extraction of non-living resources— (i) by any person in a core zone , or (ii) by a non resident in a specified use zone; (iii) by drilling for hydrocarbon exploration and exploitation; or (iv) by seabed, beaches and river beds mining. (d) production of salt by heating sea water with firewood or other hydrocarbon-based fuel; (e) construction and development in the nature of. (i) port development or dredging; (ii) industrial development ; (iii) use of explosives for any purpose; and (iv) dumping into marine waters of solid waste or chemically polluted water or liquid; (f) tourist activities involving jet skiing and use of sea planes; (g) any cultivation of farming within the buffer zone; (h) animal feeding in Mlola forest and catchment areas within the MIMP ; and (i) the burning of lime by use of snail shells locally known as tondo. Part III – Regulated activities

provisions.title: Regulated activities
provisions.content: The activities provided for under this Part shall be regulated within the MIMP in the manner and extent indicated herein.

provisions.title: Net fishing of dagaa by local residents
provisions.content: Notwithstanding the provisions of (paragraph (iii) of sub regulation (a) of regulation 3 , it shall be lawful for a local resident to engage in the fishing of dagaa with nets in a general zone where the waters exceed twenty meters in depth.

provisions.title: Sports fishing
provisions.content: (1) No person shall engage in sports fishing within the MIMP except in general use zones, having a sports fishing licence issued under the Fisheries Act , 2003 or its regulations and after obtaining a sports fishing permit from the Warden under these Regulations. (2) Any person permitted to engage in sports fishing pursuant to sub regulation (1) , shall immediately release into the sea any live fish lifted in weight range specified for the species as indicated in the sports fishing permit issued to him. (3) The form of sports fishing permit from the Warden shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Octopus fishing
provisions.content: (1) No person shall fish octopus by skin diving during neap tides. (2) No person shall lift, and no purchaser shall purchase any octopus weighing less than 0.5 kilogram. (3) The form of permit for non- resident to fish octopus shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Spine lobsters fishing
provisions.content: (1) No person shall lift or buy a spine lobster of weight below 0.5 kilogram (2) No person shall lift or buy a spine lobster which have laid eggs, as may be visible on the belly. (3) The form of permit for non-residents to fish for lobster and sea cucumber shall be as set out in the Schedule to these regulations.

provisions.title: Coral mining
provisions.content: (1) No person shall engage in the mining of coral or coral rock, whether live or dead, below the high water mark , except where— (a) a permit is issued by the Warden ; (b) the mining is undertaken in a general —use zone by a resident for his personal use; and (c) the material mined is used for construction of domestic house or communal village building within the a MIMP village and is not intended for commercial sale to any party or for any use outside the MIMP ; and (2) A permit-holder shall not remove an amount of coral or coral rock greater than the amount specified in the permit issued by the Warden. (3) A permit-holder shall not remove any amount of coral or coral rock from any location other than the location specified in the permit issued by the Warden. (4) The Warden or authorized officer shall approve all coral mining sites in collaboration with the relevant village councils; and no activity shall be approved in areas within the vicinity of important fishing grounds, beaches or other sensitive coastlines. (5) The Warden may issue or refuse to issue a permit for coral mining under this regulation, but shall be guided by the following criteria, namely— (a) whether or not the specified location is generally suitable having regard, among other things, its conservation status under the applicable zoning plan; (b) whether or not the additional Environmental Impact Assessment to the mined area is in his opinion acceptable; (c) whether or not the applicant has received a permit in the recent past; and (d) whether or not the applicant proposes to use sustainable alternative building materials alongside coral or coral rock. (6) The form of application and permit under these Regulations shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Land based fossil coral mining and collection of cowries
provisions.content: (1) Mining of fossil corals above the high water mark shall for purpose of construction and lime-making shall be permitted if— (a) the mining is undertaken by a resident within a general—use zone; and (b) a permit is issued by the Warden for that purpose. (2) The Warden or an officer under his directions shall approve every proposed mining site after consultation with the relevant village council. (3) No person other than a local resident shall acquire, or gather cowries for trade in a general use zone , save with a permit granted by the Warden. (4) A permit granted under this regulation shall be in the form set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Mud and mud bricks
provisions.content: Extraction of mud beyond sixty meters from the high water mark , for the purpose of making mud-bricks or other construction may only be undertaken by a resident in a general —use zone.

provisions.title: Mangrove cutting
provisions.content: (1) No person shall cut any mangrove trees except where— (a) the trees are located in a general use zone ; (b) the cutting is undertaken by a resident ; (c) the resulting poles are used for the construction of a domestic house, communal village building, boat or other structure within a MIMP village and shall be not sold commercially to any party or for any use outside the MIMP . (d) the purpose of the cutting is to clear access to small areas of beach or inter-tidal areas for a lawful activity; and (e) a permit for that purpose has been issued by the Warden ; (2) The Warden shall, in consultation with the relevant officer, approve all mangrove-cutting sites after consultations with the relevant village council. (3) A permit-holder shall not remove any mangrove tree or part of such a tree from any location other than the location specified in the permit issued by the Warden . (4) A permit-holder shall not remove any mangrove tree or part of such a tree in excess of the number of trees specified on the permit. (5) The Warden may apply his discretion to issue or refuse to issue a permit for mangrove cutting but he shall be guided by the following criteria, namely— (a) whether the specified location is generally suitable having regard, among other things, its conservation status under the applicable zoning plan; (b) whether or not the additional environment impact to the harvested area is in his opinion acceptable; (c) whether or not the applicant has received a permit in the recent past; and (d) whether or not the applicant has recently planted, or is committed to plant trees within his village area that will provide building poles in the future. (6) Collection of dead mangrove branches, fruits, leaves and seeds outside core zones shall not be restricted. (7) The form of permit under these Regulations shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations

provisions.title: Non-­mangrove forest products
provisions.content: (1) No person shall engage in pole-cutting of non-mangrove products except— (a) a resident in a general use zone for domestic construction; or (b) a resident with a permit in a specified use zone for domestic construction; (2) Collection of firewood, raffia fibre (ukindu) in a general use zone or specified use zone shall not be restricted. (3) No person shall light grass fires, or otherwise bum any forest products in the MIMP , save for making charcoal after obtaining a permit issued by the Warden, provided that no charcoal making shall be permitted in water catchment areas. (4) A permit issued under this regulation shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Salt making
provisions.content: (1) No person shall, other than a resident , engage in the production of salt by sun drying within a general use zone . (2) The Warden or any officer under his directions shall specify in every permit issued under this regulation, the location approved for the production of salt by sun drying. (3) A permit issued under this regulation shall be in the form set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Constru­ction
provisions.content: (1) No person shall establish any large scale processing plant or installation, whether floating, underwater or land based in any area within the MIMP . (2) Every development of a small scale processing plant or installation, including a desalination plant and processing plant for marine products or otherwise, whether floating or underwater, shall undergo an Environmental Impact Assessment scrutiny in accordance with the Environmental Management Act , 2004. (3) All new and existing constructions, including tourist lodges and other commercial outlets, jetties and mooring installations, excluding residential houses, civic buildings and public amenities, shall be subject to scrutiny in terms of the Environmental Impact Assessment. (4) Any existing construction or structure, which has not been subjected to the scrutiny prescribed by the Environmental Impact Assessment, shall be liable to such scrutiny, and the owner of such structure shall apply the necessary adjustments accordingly

provisions.title: Scientific research
provisions.content: (1) No person shall undertake any scientific research within any part of the MIMP save with the written permission of the Warden in consultation with a relevant research institutions and on such terms and conditions as he may attach. (2) Any person who is permitted to undertake research under this regulation shall pay such entry fee as may be prescribed, save under the Warden 's exemption if in his opinion the research will be of substantial benefit to the MIMP . (3) The form of application for grant of permit for scientific research under these Regulations shah as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations. (4) Report on any research findings shall be availed to the Warden .

provisions.title: Permits and licences
provisions.content: (1) The Warden may issue a permit or licence set out in the Schedule to these Regulations, subject to such conditions and directions, as he may deem necessary, and he shall give his reasons in writing for his refusal to issue a permit or licence requested by an applicant. (2) A permit or licence issued under these Regulations shall remain a property of the marine park and shall be produced on demand to the marine park officials.

provisions.title: Penalties
provisions.content: (1) Any person who contravenes the provisions of these Regulations or any direction made hereunder, commits an offence and shall on conviction be liable to a fine of not less than twenty thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than three months or to both. (2) Any object, instrument, product or thing used in the breach of any of these Regulations shall be liable to forfeiture.

provisions.title: Powers of arrest
provisions.content: In addition to the marine parks and reserves officials, members of MIMP villages appointed in writing for general or specific cases, by the relevant village councils, and approved by the Warden may exercise powers of arrest and other enforcement functions as may be determined from time to time by him, in respect of activities carried on within the MIMP

provisions.title: Honorary rangers
provisions.content: (1) The Unit Manager or the Warden may issue distinctive identity cards to honorary rangers among members of the relevant village who shall be charged with patrol duties and other enforcement operations pertaining to the laws and regulations governing marine parks and reserves. (2) The form of identity cards for enforcement officers of marine parks and reserves shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.
